channel.title: Mount Warning
channel.description:
Hi Wizards :mage:,
using useEffect() i am getting a warning when i try to set something in state .
Warning: Can't perform a React state update on an unmounted component. This is a no-op, but it indicates a memory leak in your application. To fix, cancel all subscriptions and asynchronous tasks in a useEffect cleanup function. in Unknown
**What i am trying to do?** fetching data from an external source after user is authenticated and landed on dashboard.

item.title: Mount Warning
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Dan Abramov (one of the React core devs) recommends this approach: https://github.com/facebook/react/issues/14369#issuecomment-468267798

useEffect(() => {
  let didCancel = false;
 
  // ...
  // you can check didCancel
  // before running any setState
  // ...
 
  return () => {
    didCancel = true; // This function is executed on unmount.
  };
}, []);

Read full topic

item.title: Mount Warning
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Could you please share the code of that component?

Read full topic

item.title: Mount Warning
item.dc:creator: aeyzazkhan
item.description:
Hi Wizards ,

using useEffect() i am getting a warning when i try to set something in state .

Warning: Can't perform a React state update on an unmounted component. This is a no-op, but it indicates a memory leak in your application. To fix, cancel all subscriptions and asynchronous tasks in a useEffect cleanup function.
    in Unknown

What i am trying to do?
fetching data from an external source after user is authenticated and landed on dashboard.

Read full topic
